paper_title: Hydrological Processes and Isotopic Signatures in the Ghaggar River Basin

methods.description: This method involves the collection and analysis of water samples to document spatial and vertical variation in isotopic signatures (d 18 O, d 2 H, and 3 H) of source water and groundwater across the study area.

steps.description: Water sampling was carried out throughout the study area during June and July 2013 for d 18 O and d 2 H analysis, and October-November 2012, June 2013, and April and June 2015 for 3 H analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water samples for isotopic analysis

steps.description: Collected groundwater samples from 244 locations from monitoring wells, public tube wells, and hand pumps using a grid of about 10 x 10 km across the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: collect groundwater samples from specified locations

steps.description: The wells were purged for 30-45 min depending on the depth of the well before sampling.
Canal and river water samples were collected from random locations based on accessibility within the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: collect canal and river water samples

steps.description: Three rain gauge stations were set up in Chandigarh, Patiala, and Sirsa to develop a local meteoric water line for the Ghaggar River basin.
All water samples were collected in pre-cleaned polypropylene bottles, rinsed at the site twice with the sample water, tightly sealed, and brought immediately to the laboratory for isotopic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and transport water samples for analysis

steps.description: Stable isotopic (d 2 H, d 18 O) and tritium (3 H) analyses were carried out at the Nuclear Hydrology Laboratory at NIH Roorkee, India.
steps.searchable_summary: perform isotopic analysis at NIH Roorkee

steps.description: Measurements of d 18 O and d 2 H were made using Continuous Flow Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometry and Dual Inlet Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometry following standard procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: measure isotopic ratios using mass spectrometry
outputs: d 18 O and d 2 H data

steps.description: Tritium activity in enriched samples was measured using an ultra-low-level liquid scintillation counter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tritium activity using scintillation counter

methods.description: Development of a conceptual model for the hydrological processes operating in the Ghaggar Basin, considering geomorphic settings, isotopic variations, and groundwater flow directions.

steps.description: Use information on geomorphic setting, spatial variability of isotopic signatures, tritium concentration, aquifer-thickness logs, and groundwater flow directions to develop a conceptual model.
Analyze depth-wise isotopic data to suggest local, intermediate, and regional flow patterns in the Ghaggar Basin.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze isotopic data for flow patterns

steps.description: Interpret major lateral and vertical variations in groundwater isotopic signatures as due to heterogeneous distribution of aquifer and non-aquifer sediments.
